settings.title: Introducing the Rumi Collection
settings.description: As we unveil our newest collection Rumi, we sat down with our Head Designer, Shona MacDonald, to explore the deeper meaning behind the collection.

settings.content_intro:
DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.description:
'Rumi' translates to "current of flow", symbolic of a shifting and evolving journey. Tell us how this resonated with you and led to the Rumi Collection?

This collection has been the perfect tale of the creative process. The ability to pause, reflect, evolve. Born out of an idea for a different project, Rumi grew into a fun and free expression of creativity. Sometimes as creatives it can be hard to let go and just go back to a creative state of play. Rumi embodies that for us.


In crafting this collection there was a process of allowing the natural path to present itself, numerous trials to achieve the ideal flow. Tell us about this creative process and how it challenged our jewellers?

The way that Rumi evolved meant leaning into the development of the designs. Taking the extra time to do multiple trials, and allow our jewellers the freedom to explore. I believe that telling a creative to make something look effortless, or as if it happened by accident is one of the hardest tasks to achieve - but when you do achieve it, it is a wonder of sorts, and something for our team to take great pride in.

With Rumi we really want to create something that emulated that sense of the beauty found in happy accidents, the beauty of nature, the fluidity of lifes journey. It took a lot of trial and error to get the curves and the flow of the band just right. Meticulous hours upon end playing around with groups of diamonds to find the perfect direction for our Rumi Ring | Diamond.

settings.description:
What was something unexpected that arose from crafting this collection?

Our Rumi Ring Set was a very unplanned addition to the collection. Sometimes, when you're working on a collection, an idea just comes from nowhere and suddenly feels like it's of the deepest important to bring it to life. This piece felt like the finishing touch.

Two bands, physically held together by a linking charm - the perfect summation to the moments in life that stop us in our tracks, and change us for the better.

settings.title: Rumi
settings.description:
Water flows. Never staying put, it surrenders.

Rumi is designed to be stacked and made to be lived in, moulding perfectly to the wearer’s life.

What is your most-loved piece from the collection?

I'm obsessed with the Rumi Ring | Diamond, especially two stacked together. I love the way you can play with this collection and express your creativity, layering pieces together to make your own unique stack and tell your own story. There are also 68 diamonds in this piece, some as small as 0.8mm, which create the undulating flow of diamonds across the surface of the ring. A labour of love for our setters, but such a beautiful exuberant summary of the joy we poured into this collection.

settings.title: "The perfect summation to the moments in life that stop us in our tracks, and change us for the better."

settings.description:
This collection was designed to be styled and stacked, customised with birthstone pieces and invites our customers to tell their story. Tell us a little bit about why this was important for this collection specifically.

There is nothing I love more than when we get to interact with our customers. I feel honoured to be invited into our communities lives, to assist them in telling their stories, and expressing themselves in a multitude of different ways.

I hope that the variety of options found within the Rumi collection allow you to either build upon these pieces as your life evolves and shifts, or customise them to represent people or milestones already present in your life. I can't wait to see how different people interact with the pieces in this collection, styling if with their existing pieces, and building Rumi stacks of their own.

To pass the opportunity for this collection to continue to develop, outside of NMJ fills me with excitement for what it could become and the significance it could hold for people. I hope it invites our community to take time to be present and mark the special moments in life, as they pass by so fast, it's important to sit in the feelings once in a while and I hope Rumi can encourage this.


Tell us your hopes for this collection.

I hope this collection brings the same opportunity for creative play to our community, as it did for me. Sometimes, we just need to open up to creativity and embrace the opportunity to lean in and take a chance. Rumi brought such joy to us as a team as we watched it evolve and develop, and we hope it does the same for you.
